mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, labor market deterioration signals severe economic contraction, causing investors to rapidly de-risk and driving negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand recovery, generating sustained positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a joint regression coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects genuine supply-side expansion without overheating, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or the mechanism shows negative predictive accuracy in stable inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth surpassing 2.0% confirms robust aggregate demand, prompting immediate equity market repricing and generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week of data release.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%, or the signal exhibits zero autocorrelation with market direction.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm genuine labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS rise and UNRATE fall fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 52% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly forces expectations of central bank tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the inflation threshold shows no statistical link to subsequent rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising personal consumption signals supply chain bottlenecks and inventory depletion, triggering short-term market volatility and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with predictive accuracy indistinguishable from random chance (50%) across multiple market cycles.
